posts.title: Regional Security Through Economic Means
posts.excerpt: On the central insight of the U.S. 2025 National Strategy — and what it means in practice for governments in the hemisphere.

posts.title: The Local Economy First
posts.excerpt: Why national strategies that ignore community-level economic stability tend to fail — and how leaders can sequence growth so it is felt where citizens live.

posts.title: A New Mandate in Latin America
posts.excerpt: Across the region, populations are electing leaders willing to plan and execute strategically. What that opening requires of the leaders who accept it.

posts.title: Assess, Develop, Execute
posts.excerpt: The three-stage discipline at the heart of nation building — and the predictable failure modes when any stage is skipped or rushed.

posts.title: From the Author's Desk
posts.excerpt: Excerpts and notes from John deVerteuil's published work on national strategy and the practical architecture of nation building.

posts.title: Standing With the Leaders
posts.excerpt: On what it means to assist a national leadership in every aspect of their future — and the obligations that go with that posture.
